nativeRange.habitat: Outer reef slopes and current-swept drop-offs, generally reported at depths of around 3–40 m.
nativeRange.biotope: Outer reef slopes with moderate to strong current

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and overhangs

diet.notes: Planktivorous in the wild; generally takes frozen mysis, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, copepods and amphipods in captivity, and over time tends to accept high-quality pellets and flake foods. Multiple small feedings throughout the day are commonly advised, and a refugium seeded with pods is often recommended.

summary: A small, fiery red-and-yellow Indo-Pacific anthias generally kept as a single male with several females over open rockwork in well-oxygenated reef tanks.
careNotes: Often sold under the trade name 'Flame Anthias' alongside the Ignitus Anthias label — both refer to Pseudanthias ignitus, described by Randall and Lubbock (1981) from the Indo-West Pacific (Maldives, Andaman Islands, Indonesia, Thailand's Similan Islands). It is a protogynous hermaphrodite (all individuals born female; the dominant female of a group will tend to transition into a male if the resident male is lost) and is best kept as a single male with multiple females — keeping two males together has been reported to trigger extreme aggression. LiveAquaria suggests a 70 US gal (~265 L) minimum for a single specimen and ~125 US gal (~473 L) for a harem of one male plus up to ten females; saltwaterfish.com also cites 70 US gal. Minimum tank rounded up to 300 L for a single fish; a larger 500 L+ system is generally advised for harems with adequate swimming space. Cross-source notes on adult size: FishBase and LiveAquaria both record about 9 cm / 3.5 in TL, while saltwaterfish.com cites 4 in (10 cm) — recorded here as 8–9 cm. Temperature range averaged across LiveAquaria (~22–26 °C, converted from 72–78 °F), saltwaterfish.com (~23–27 °C) and FishBase (22–27 °C) to 22–26 °C. Like most anthias this species is generally considered an escape risk and a tight-fitting lid is advised. Heavy planktivore metabolism: multiple feedings per day are commonly recommended, and the species tends to do poorly in tanks without a steady supply of small particulate food.
breedingNotes: Rarely bred in captivity. Protogynous hermaphrodite forming wild harems on outer reef slopes; almost all aquarium stock is wild-collected. When courting, the male's coloration intensifies and the dorsal fin is held erect.

additional.name: saltwaterfish.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked minimum tank size (70 US gal), water parameters (74–80 °F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025), peaceful temperament with male-male aggression caveat, diet, and protogynous hermaphroditism. Listed adult size 4 in (10 cm) — disagrees slightly with LiveAquaria/FishBase 3.5 in / 9 cm.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Anthiadidae, Perciformes), authorship (Randall & Lubbock 1981), max size (9.0 cm TL), native range (Indo-West Pacific: Andaman Islands, Maldives, Indonesia, Similan Islands), depth (10–40 m), and temperature (22–27 °C). Designated secondary Reef2Reef wiki was not used — saltwaterfish.com served as the discovery-supplied secondary and FishBase added an authoritative taxonomic confirmation.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding is rarely reported; sourcing is virtually all wild-collected.
